factor_rows.rationale: Repeated clean evidence, positive net edge, and no toxic opportunity-cost companion mean this factor is ready for first-pass evaluation now.
The factor repeats and carries positive edge, but at least one pocket ties it to toxic opportunity cost or negative pocket-level net edge.
Current evidence is still too thin or too local to justify formal evaluation beyond registry preservation.

interpretation: A factor qualifies for first-pass evaluation only when repeated evidence remains clean after pocket-level opportunity-cost checks.
Penalty-track factors should still be evaluated, but only under an explicit opportunity-cost adjustment rather than as clean retained alpha candidates.
Thin factors should stay in the registry until a later batch adds more repeated evidence.
